1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم تبارك الذي بيده الملك وهو على كل شيء قدير
1.english: Blessed is He in whose hand is dominion, and He is over all things competent -
1.transliteration: Tabaarakal lazee biyadihil mulku wa huwa 'alaa kulli shai-in qadeer

1.arabic: الذي خلق الموت والحياة ليبلوكم أيكم أحسن عملا ۚ وهو العزيز الغفور
1.english: [He] who created death and life to test you [as to] which of you is best in deed - and He is the Exalted in Might, the Forgiving -
1.transliteration: Allazee khalaqal mawta walhayaata liyabluwakum ayyukum ahsanu 'amalaa; wa huwal 'azeezul ghafoor

1.arabic: الذي خلق سبع سماوات طباقا ۖ ما ترى في خلق الرحمن من تفاوت ۖ فارجع البصر هل ترى من فطور
1.english: [And] who created seven heavens in layers. You do not see in the creation of the Most Merciful any inconsistency. So return [your] vision [to the sky]; do you see any breaks?
1.transliteration: Allazee khalaqa sab'a samaawaatin tibaaqam maa taraa fee khalqir rahmaani min tafaawutin farji'il basara hal taraa min futoor

1.arabic: ثم ارجع البصر كرتين ينقلب إليك البصر خاسئا وهو حسير
1.english: Then return [your] vision twice again. [Your] vision will return to you humbled while it is fatigued.
1.transliteration: Summar ji'il basara karrataini yanqalib ilaikal basaru khaasi'anw wa huwa haseer

1.arabic: ولقد زينا السماء الدنيا بمصابيح وجعلناها رجوما للشياطين ۖ وأعتدنا لهم عذاب السعير
1.english: And We have certainly beautified the nearest heaven with stars and have made [from] them what is thrown at the devils and have prepared for them the punishment of the Blaze.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad zaiyannas samaaa'ad dunyaa bimasaa beeha wa ja'alnaahaa rujoomal lish shayaateeni wa a'tadnaa lahum 'azaabas sa'eer

1.arabic: وللذين كفروا بربهم عذاب جهنم ۖ وبئس المصير
1.english: And for those who disbelieved in their Lord is the punishment of Hell, and wretched is the destination.
1.transliteration: Wa lillazeena kafaroo bi rabbihim 'azaabu jahannama wa bi'sal maseer

1.arabic: إذا ألقوا فيها سمعوا لها شهيقا وهي تفور
1.english: When they are thrown into it, they hear from it a [dreadful] inhaling while it boils up.
1.transliteration: Izaaa ulqoo feehaa sami'oo lahaa shaheeqanw wa hiya tafoor

1.arabic: تكاد تميز من الغيظ ۖ كلما ألقي فيها فوج سألهم خزنتها ألم يأتكم نذير
1.english: It almost bursts with rage. Every time a company is thrown into it, its keepers ask them, "Did there not come to you a warner?"
1.transliteration: Takaadu tamayyazu minal ghaizi kullamaaa uliqya feehaa fawjun sa alahum khazanatuhaaa alam yaatikum nazeer

1.arabic: قالوا بلى قد جاءنا نذير فكذبنا وقلنا ما نزل الله من شيء إن أنتم إلا في ضلال كبير
1.english: They will say," Yes, a warner had come to us, but we denied and said, 'Allah has not sent down anything. You are not but in great error.'"
1.transliteration: Qaaloo balaa qad jaaa'anaa nazeerun fakazzabnaa wa qulnaa maa nazzalal laahu min shai in in antum illaa fee dalaalin kabeer

1.arabic: وقالوا لو كنا نسمع أو نعقل ما كنا في أصحاب السعير
1.english: And they will say, "If only we had been listening or reasoning, we would not be among the companions of the Blaze."
1.transliteration: Wa qaaloo law kunnaa nasma'u awna'qilu maa kunnaa feee as haabis sa'eer

1.arabic: فاعترفوا بذنبهم فسحقا لأصحاب السعير
1.english: And they will admit their sin, so [it is] alienation for the companions of the Blaze.
1.transliteration: Fa'tarafoo bizambihim fasuhqal li as haabis sa'eer

1.arabic: إن الذين يخشون ربهم بالغيب لهم مغفرة وأجر كبير
1.english: Indeed, those who fear their Lord unseen will have forgiveness and great reward.
1.transliteration: Innal lazeena yakhshawna rabbahum bilghaibi lahum maghfiratunw wa ajrun kabeer

1.arabic: وأسروا قولكم أو اجهروا به ۖ إنه عليم بذات الصدور
1.english: And conceal your speech or publicize it; indeed, He is Knowing of that within the breasts.
1.transliteration: Wa asirroo qawlakum awijharoo bihee innahoo 'aleemum bizaatis sudoor

1.arabic: ألا يعلم من خلق وهو اللطيف الخبير
1.english: Does He who created not know, while He is the Subtle, the Acquainted?
1.transliteration: Alaa ya'lamu man khalaq wa huwal lateeful khabeer

1.arabic: هو الذي جعل لكم الأرض ذلولا فامشوا في مناكبها وكلوا من رزقه ۖ وإليه النشور
1.english: It is He who made the earth tame for you - so walk among its slopes and eat of His provision - and to Him is the resurrection.
1.transliteration: Huwal lazee ja'ala lakumul arda zaloolan famshoo fee manaakibihaa wa kuloo mir rizqihee wa ilaihin nushoor

1.arabic: أأمنتم من في السماء أن يخسف بكم الأرض فإذا هي تمور
1.english: Do you feel secure that He who [holds authority] in the heaven would not cause the earth to swallow you and suddenly it would sway?
1.transliteration: 'A-amintum man fissamaaa'i aiyakhsifa bi kumul arda fa izaa hiya tamoor

1.arabic: أم أمنتم من في السماء أن يرسل عليكم حاصبا ۖ فستعلمون كيف نذير
1.english: Or do you feel secure that He who [holds authority] in the heaven would not send against you a storm of stones? Then you would know how [severe] was My warning.
1.transliteration: Am amintum man fissamaaa'i ai yursila 'alaikum haasiban fasata'lamoona kaifa nazeer

1.arabic: ولقد كذب الذين من قبلهم فكيف كان نكير
1.english: And already had those before them denied, and how [terrible] was My reproach.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad kazzabal lazeena min qablihim fakaifa kaana nakeer

1.arabic: أولم يروا إلى الطير فوقهم صافات ويقبضن ۚ ما يمسكهن إلا الرحمن ۚ إنه بكل شيء بصير
1.english: Do they not see the birds above them with wings outspread and [sometimes] folded in? None holds them [aloft] except the Most Merciful. Indeed He is, of all things, Seeing.
1.transliteration: Awalam yaraw ilat tairi fawqahum saaaffaatinw wa yaqbidn; maa yumsikuhunna illaar rahmaan; innahoo bikulli shai im baseer

1.arabic: أمن هذا الذي هو جند لكم ينصركم من دون الرحمن ۚ إن الكافرون إلا في غرور
1.english: Or who is it that could be an army for you to aid you other than the Most Merciful? The disbelievers are not but in delusion.
1.transliteration: Amman haazal lazee huwa jundul lakum yansurukum min doonir rahmaan; inilkaafiroona illaa fee ghuroor

1.arabic: أمن هذا الذي يرزقكم إن أمسك رزقه ۚ بل لجوا في عتو ونفور
1.english: Or who is it that could provide for you if He withheld His provision? But they have persisted in insolence and aversion.
1.transliteration: Amman haazal lazee yarzuqukum in amsaka rizqah; bal lajjoo fee 'utuwwinw wa nufoor

1.arabic: أفمن يمشي مكبا على وجهه أهدى أمن يمشي سويا على صراط مستقيم
1.english: Then is one who walks fallen on his face better guided or one who walks erect on a straight path?
1.transliteration: Afamai yamshee mukibban 'alaa wajhihee ahdaaa ammany yamshee sawiyyan 'alaa siratim mustaqeem

1.arabic: قل هو الذي أنشأكم وجعل لكم السمع والأبصار والأفئدة ۖ قليلا ما تشكرون
1.english: Say, "It is He who has produced you and made for you hearing and vision and hearts; little are you grateful."
1.transliteration: Qul huwal lazee ansha akum wa ja'ala lakumus sam'a wal absaara wal af'idata qaleelam maa tashkuroon

1.arabic: قل هو الذي ذرأكم في الأرض وإليه تحشرون
1.english: Say, "It is He who has multiplied you throughout the earth, and to Him you will be gathered."
1.transliteration: Qul huwal lazee zara akum fil ardi wa ilaihi tuhsharoon

1.arabic: ويقولون متى هذا الوعد إن كنتم صادقين
1.english: And they say, "When is this promise, if you should be truthful?"
1.transliteration: Wa yaqooloona mataa haazal wa'du in kuntum saadiqeen

1.arabic: قل إنما العلم عند الله وإنما أنا نذير مبين
1.english: Say, "The knowledge is only with Allah, and I am only a clear warner."
1.transliteration: Qul innamal 'ilmu 'indallaahi wa innamaaa ana nazeerum mubeen

1.arabic: فلما رأوه زلفة سيئت وجوه الذين كفروا وقيل هذا الذي كنتم به تدعون
1.english: But when they see it approaching, the faces of those who disbelieve will be distressed, and it will be said, "This is that for which you used to call."
1.transliteration: Falaammaa ra-awhu zulfatan seee'at wujoohul lazeena kafaroo wa qeela haazal lazee kuntum bihee tadda'oon

1.arabic: قل أرأيتم إن أهلكني الله ومن معي أو رحمنا فمن يجير الكافرين من عذاب أليم
1.english: Say, [O Muhammad], "Have you considered: whether Allah should cause my death and those with me or have mercy upon us, who can protect the disbelievers from a painful punishment?"
1.transliteration: Qul ara'aytum in ahlaka niyal laahu wa mam ma'iya aw rahimanaa famai-yujeerul kaafireena min 'azaabin aleem

1.arabic: قل هو الرحمن آمنا به وعليه توكلنا ۖ فستعلمون من هو في ضلال مبين
1.english: Say, "He is the Most Merciful; we have believed in Him, and upon Him we have relied. And you will [come to] know who it is that is in clear error."
1.transliteration: Qul huwar rahmaanu aamannaa bihee wa 'alaihi tawakkalnaa fasata'lamoona man huwa fee dalaalim mubeen

1.arabic: قل أرأيتم إن أصبح ماؤكم غورا فمن يأتيكم بماء معين
1.english: Say, "Have you considered: if your water was to become sunken [into the earth], then who could bring you flowing water?"
1.transliteration: Qul ara'aytum in asbaha maaa'ukum ghawran famai yaateekum bimaaa'im ma'een
